summarization.a: The figure shows the film thickness of a material over cycles, with a plateau at around 200 Å until cycle 60, followed by a linear decrease at a rate of 3.1 Å per cycle.
summarization.b: The figure shows a zoomed-in view of the subfigure a, between 20 and 40 pulses/cycles.

a.question: Which are the main steps shown in the figure?
a.answer: First, the substrates were exposed to 30 pulses of SF6 plasma (Half-cycle A), followed by 30 pulses of TMA (half-cycle B), and finally 50 ALE cycles (SF6 plasma + TMA dose).

a.question: Which reactant exhibits a slight increase in the film thickness?
a.answer: The exposure to SF6 plasma, which is related to the formation of a thin fluorinated layer.

a.question: How does  the film thickness change over the course of the full ALE cycle?
a.answer: The film thickness is decreasing with an etch rate of 3.1 Å per cycle.

b.question: How does the film thickness change after half-cycle A?
b.answer: After the half-cycle A, the film was exposed to a TMA dose, resulting in a decrease in thickness from 207 Å to 204 Å.
